evidence.title: Associated Press analysis: Israel advances on Syrian buffer zone, temporary/tactical framing, 9 Dec. 2024
evidence.source_description: Associated Press page opened with web tool on 2026-06-29; relevant article text extracted from rendered lines 86-176. Source is credible contemporaneous reporting including UN and Israeli official characterizations.
evidence.relevance: Adverse and contextual evidence for the defense: AP reported Israel described the buffer-zone seizure as temporary and security-driven; quoted an Israeli military official that it was not trying to change the border or prepare an invasion into Syria; also confirms UN/UNDOF violation framing and therefore preserves both sides of the characterization dispute.

evidence.title: Israel PMO Mount Hermon statement, 17 Dec. 2024
evidence.source_description: Israel government Prime Minister's Office page identified by web search/open; direct local fetch returned HTTP 403, so this is a concise capture of visible indexed page text returned by web search on 2026-06-29.
evidence.relevance: Official Israeli statement placing Netanyahu at the summit of Mount Hermon on 17 Dec. 2024, receiving operational briefing, issuing directives on continued IDF deployment, and saying Israel would stay/deploy there until another arrangement ensures security.

evidence.title: Israel PMO statement on taking control of Syria buffer zone, 12 Dec. 2024
evidence.source_description: Israel government Prime Minister's Office page identified by web search/open; direct local fetch returned HTTP 403, so this is a concise capture of the visible indexed page text returned by web search on 2026-06-29.
evidence.relevance: Official Israeli statement that Netanyahu ordered the IDF to temporarily take control of the buffer zone in Syria until an effective force enforces the 1974 Separation of Forces Agreement; supports Israeli official confirmation of control, while preserving the defensive/temporary framing.

evidence.title: Jerusalem Post report: Israel takes over Syrian side of Mount Hermon, 8 Dec. 2024
evidence.source_description: Jerusalem Post article locally fetched and text-extracted 2026-06-29.
evidence.relevance: Credible contemporaneous reporting that on 8 Dec. 2024 the IDF took over the Syrian side of Mount Hermon to expand a demilitarized buffer zone, with IDF photo caption from the Syrian area of Mount Hermon.

evidence.title: Israel PMO Golan Heights statement, 8 Dec. 2024
evidence.source_description: Israel government Prime Minister's Office page identified by web search/open; direct local fetch returned HTTP 403, so this is a concise capture of visible indexed page text returned by web search on 2026-06-29.
evidence.relevance: Official Israeli statement on 8 Dec. 2024 that Israel ordered the army to take over positions beyond its border in Syria, while characterizing the move as temporary and defensive; relevant to both control and adverse characterization.

evidence.title: Ynet report: Netanyahu visits Syrian side of Mount Hermon, 17 Dec. 2024
evidence.source_description: Ynet News article locally fetched and text-extracted 2026-06-29.
evidence.relevance: Credible reporting that Netanyahu visited the Syrian side of Mount Hermon on 17 Dec. 2024 and said Israel and Israeli troops would remain until another arrangement guaranteed security; corroborates official PMO statement and location.

evidence.title: UNDOF Press Statement, 13 Dec. 2024
evidence.source_description: United Nations Peacekeeping webpage, locally fetched 2026-06-29; extracted text from page body/meta.
evidence.relevance: Official UN/UNDOF confirmation that since 7 Dec. 2024 there were significant IDF movements within the area of separation and along the ceasefire line, that IDF remained in the area of separation in multiple locations as of 13 Dec., and that UNDOF told Israel these actions violated the 1974 Disengagement Agreement.
